text: The circular stapler is gently inserted through the anus and advanced through the rectum, with the assistant stretching the rectum to facilitate the insertion.
The circular stapler is positioned under visual control, and its spike perforates the bowel in the central third, just below the previously described staple line.
The surgeon approximates the anvil and connects it to the circular stapler, preparing for the anastomosis in the rectal cancer procedure.
The surgeon releases tension on the stapler, allowing the two lateral thirds of the linear staple line to approximate below the axis of the anvil, forming a reverse smile shape.
The surgeon closes the circular stapler completely, encompassing the linear staple line within its closure.
The assistant maintains the linear staple line wrapped around the anvil, aiding in the positioning and alignment during the stapling process.
The surgeon displays images showing two complete tissue donuts, indicating successful circular stapling in the rectal cancer procedure.
